fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: New Jersey Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as an occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to New Jersey - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity new jersey, how to transfer occupational therapist license to new jersey, occupational therapist compact new jersey, new jersey occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: New Jersey Board of Occupational Therapy
board.parent_dept: New Jersey Division of Consumer Affairs
board.mailing_address: New Jersey Board of Occupational Therapy Examiners, P.O. Box 45042, Newark, New Jersey 07101

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) examination

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in New Jersey.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Register and Apply Online
steps.description: Create an account on the New Jersey Division of Consumer Affairs - Online Licensing Services portal, log in, and complete the online application for Occupational Therapist licensure.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts to be sent directly from your ACOTE-accredited program to the Board, and ensure your NBCOT OTR exam scores are sent directly from NBCOT to the Board.

steps.title: Complete Criminal History Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided within the New Jersey Division of Consumer Affairs - Online Licensing Services portal to complete the required fingerprinting for a criminal history background check.

steps.title: Provide Certifications and Authorizations
steps.description: Submit any necessary certification and authorization forms as requested during the online application process.

steps.title: Pay Application and Initial Licensure Fees
steps.description: Pay the non-refundable application fee and the initial licensure fee (which varies based on the biennial cycle) through the New Jersey Division of Consumer Affairs - Online Licensing Services portal.

steps.title: Await Board Review and License Issuance
steps.description: The Board will review all submitted materials and verifications. Once approved, your Occupational Therapist license will be issued, and you will be notified.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: New Jersey offers a temporary occupational therapy permit for new graduates who have completed an ACOTE-accredited program and are awaiting their NBCOT exam results. The temporary permit allows practice under the direct supervision of a licensed OTR. Contact the New Jersey Board of Occupational Therapy for current temporary permit requirements and duration limits.
reciprocity_text: Occupational therapists licensed in another state may apply for licensure in New Jersey through endorsement. Submit a completed application to the New Jersey Board of Occupational Therapy, along with official license verification from your current state and proof of active NBCOT certification. Processing typically takes 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: How much does a New Jersey OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial application and licensing fee is approximately $120. Check the board website for the latest fee schedule.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does New Jersey have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers occupational therapist practice in New Jersey. To work in New Jersey, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to New Jersey?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in New Jersey while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Occupational therapists licensed in another state may apply for licensure in New Jersey through endorsement. Submit a completed application to the New Jersey Board of Occupational Therapy, along with official license verification from your current state and proof of active NBCOT certification. Processing typically takes 4 to 6 Weeks.

hero.title: Become an Occupational Therapist in New Jersey
hero.dek: To apply for an Occupational Therapist license in New Jersey, register and submit your application through the New Jersey Division of Consumer Affairs - Online Licensing Services portal.

variants.label: Renewal

official_links.portal_name: New Jersey Division of Consumer Affairs - Online Licensing Services
official_links.registration_steps: Click 'First Time Users' on the portal homepage, agree to the terms, provide personal information (name, DOB, SSN, email, phone), create a username and password, and answer security questions to complete registration. Once registered, log in and select 'Apply for a New License'.
